symptoms.symptom_name: Dysuria — severe burning/stinging pain on micturition

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dysuria is the cardinal symptom of lower UTI.
Urethritis also causes dysuria but usually without prominent suprapubic symptoms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary frequency and urgency with persistent urge despite empty bladder

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Detrusor irritability from bladder-wall inflammation produces frequency/urgency of small volumes.
Suprapubic discomfort localises inflammation to the bladder rather than the kidney or urethra alone.
Pyuria and bacteriuria make urine cloudy and malodorous; supportive but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors and flank/loin pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic toxicity and loin pain are the hallmark of upper-tract infection; their absence argues strongly against pyelonephritis.
Infection confined to the bladder without systemic features supports an uncomplicated lower UTI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of nausea/vomiting; tolerating oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to tolerate oral therapy supports outpatient oral management and argues against a systemic/upper-tract infection needing IV therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of vaginal discharge or external irritation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of discharge/itch and localisation to micturition makes vaginitis unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic tenderness on palpation with absent costovertebral angle tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised suprapubic tenderness with NO loin tenderness is the examination signature of bladder-confined infection — a sealed clinician finding, not available in telehealth.
Absent costovertebral angle tenderness argues against renal involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine dipstick positive for leukocyte esterase and nitrites

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Leukocyte esterase (pyuria) plus nitrites (nitrate-reducing bacteriuria) strongly support bacterial UTI — a sealed on-site clinician test, not obtainable in a chat-only telehealth encounter.
Culture is the definitive confirmation and guides antibiotic choice if empiric therapy fails, but is not required before starting empiric treatment in an uncomplicated case.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dysuria + frequency + urgency + suprapubic discomfort with cloudy urine is the classic uncomplicated cystitis cluster in a non-pregnant woman.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the presumptive diagnosis where available; in an uncomplicated case empiric therapy may be started on symptoms alone. Not obtainable in a chat-only telehealth encounter.
Reserve for treatment failure, recurrence, pregnancy, or diagnostic uncertainty; guides antibiotic choice by sensitivities.
Not indicated for a first uncomplicated lower UTI; over-investigation. Consider only for recurrent infection, suspected obstruction, or male/complicated cases.
Not indicated without systemic features; reserved for suspected pyelonephritis or urosepsis.
